items.question: A colleague plots accuracy for three models ['A','B','C'] as a connected line. What is wrong and what should they use?
options: Line implies order between unordered categories; use a bar chart
Use a heatmap of one row
items.answer: Line implies order between unordered categories; use a bar chart
items.explanation: Model names have no natural sequence; connecting them suggests a false trend. Bar charts compare discrete categories on a common baseline.

items.question: Which chart best answers "What is the shape of the age feature distribution?"
options: Scatter of age vs row index
Line chart of sorted age values
items.explanation: One numeric variable, distribution-shape question → histogram. Scatter vs index does not show frequency structure.

items.question: For comparing inference latency distributions across five deployment regions, the best default is:
options: Bar chart of mean latency only
Box plot of latency by region
items.answer: Box plot of latency by region
items.explanation: Numeric-by-categorical comparison of spread and outliers → box plot. Bar of means hides variance critical for SLA review.

items.question: Which topic owns the pyplot API and ax.scatter syntax—not the decision of scatter vs hist?
items.explanation: Choosing chart types owns the question-to-family framework; Matplotlib implements scatter, hist, bar, and related Axes methods.

items.question: After computing df.corr() on numeric features, which chart family gives the best matrix overview?
options: Bar chart of correlation values in random order
items.explanation: Square symmetric matrix → heatmap for at-a-glance structure. Scatter matrix is follow-up detail for selected pairs, not the first overview.
